title: PostgreSQL database passwords should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: PostgreSQL connection URL with embedded password detected - CRITICAL. This connection string contains authentication credentials in plaintext format (postgresql://user:password@host or postgres://user:password@host), providing direct database access. Exposing this allows unauthorized parties to execute SQL queries, read sensitive data, modify records, drop tables, and potentially compromise the entire database system. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1) Remove password from connection string, 2) Rotate database credentials immediately, 3) Store credentials securely using environment variables (DATABASE_URL), secrets managers (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault), or connection parameter files (.pgpass), 4) Review database access logs for unauthorized activity, 5) Implement least-privilege access controls, 6) Enable database audit logging.

PostgreSQL JDBC connection string with embedded password detected - CRITICAL. JDBC URLs with password parameters (jdbc:postgresql://host:port/database?password=xxx) expose database credentials in Java application code or configuration files. This grants full database access including read, write, and administrative operations on PostgreSQL databases. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1) Remove password parameter from JDBC URL, 2) Rotate compromised database credentials, 3) Use secure credential management (environment variables, JNDI DataSource, Spring configuration with encrypted properties, or connection parameter files), 4) Review database access logs for suspicious activity, 5) Implement connection pooling with secure credential handling.

PostgreSQL password variable assignment detected - CRITICAL. Hardcoded PostgreSQL database password found in variable assignment (postgres_password = 'xxx' or postgresql_passwd = 'xxx'). This exposes database authentication credentials in source code, allowing unauthorized access to PostgreSQL databases with full privileges. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1) Remove hardcoded password from source code, 2) Rotate exposed database credentials immediately, 3) Store passwords in environment variables (PGPASSWORD, DATABASE_PASSWORD), secrets managers (AWS/Vault/Azure), or .pgpass file, 4) Audit database logs for unauthorized access, 5) Implement principle of least privilege, 6) Enable PostgreSQL connection logging.
